fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Utah Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Utah - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity utah, how to transfer pharmacist license to utah, pharmacist compact utah, utah pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Utah Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address:
Utah Division of Professional Licensing
Heber M. Wells Building, 4th Floor
160 East 300 South
P.O. Box 146741
Salt Lake City, UT 84114-6741

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Utah.

steps.title: Create a Utah.gov ID and Apply Online
steps.description: Create an account on DOPL's Online License System, complete the online Pharmacist License application (either by examination or endorsement), and pay the non-refundable application fee.

steps.title: Request Official Transcripts
steps.description: Have your official Pharm.D. transcripts sent directly from your ACPE-accredited college of pharmacy to the Utah Division of Professional Licensing (DOPL).

steps.title: Register with NABP and Pass Examinations
steps.description: Register with the National Association of Boards of Pharmacy (NABP) to take and pass both the NAPLEX and the Utah Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE). Ensure your scores are reported directly to the Utah Division of Professional Licensing.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by DOPL after application submission to complete the required fingerprint-based criminal background check.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Utah pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Utah have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Utah. To work in Utah, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Utah?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Utah while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Utah
hero.dek: To apply for a Pharmacist license in Utah, you must submit your application and required documentation through DOPL's Online License System.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account and start your application, visit the DOPL's Online License System login page. Click 'Create an Account' if you don't have a Utah.gov ID and follow the prompts to register, verify your email, and set up your credentials. Once logged in, navigate to 'Apply for a New License' and select 'Pharmacist'.
